primary_question: How should ProductOS upgrade the presentation capability so it becomes decision-grade?
decision_required: Approve the current presentation-system scope and publishing model.
recommendation: Adopt a narrative rendering pipeline with explicit evidence, story, and publish-check stages.
audience_key_question: How should ProductOS upgrade the presentation capability so it becomes decision-grade?
opening_answer: Treat presentation creation as a narrative and evidence operating system, not a styling feature.
main_recommendation: Approve the current presentation-system scope and publishing model.
objections_addressed: Why is stronger styling alone insufficient?
How do we keep better storytelling from overstating certainty?
story_arc: Lead with the decision and why the current pipeline is structurally limited.
Show the evidence for where fidelity and narrative quality break down.
Close with the bounded current presentation operating model.
story_transitions: Build from PM Operating System Update into Where We Are without breaking the recommendation thread.
Build from Where We Are into What Still Needs Work without breaking the recommendation thread.

slides.title: PM Operating System Update
slides.headline: The presentation system should become a narrative renderer.
slides.core_message: The next version needs better thinking, evidence, and publishing controls, not just better slide styling.
supporting_points: The current system renders quickly but compresses too much structure too early.
A new story layer can preserve decisions, evidence, and notes.
slides.speaker_notes: Set the frame: the capability problem is architectural, not cosmetic.
slides.visual_direction: Hero statement with a short recommendation strip.
slides.audience_question_answered: What decision should leadership make about the presentation capability now?
slides.claim: The presentation system should be funded as a narrative rendering system, not treated as a visual polish pass.
slides.why_now: Leadership should approve the current presentation-system investment and understand why this is a trust-and-execution upgrade rather than cosmetic work.
slides.proof_strategy: State the decision clearly; Show why the current architecture, not just the current styling, is the real constraint

slides.title: Where We Are
slides.headline: The platform already has strong product source artifacts.
slides.core_message: The weak output comes from a thin presentation grammar, not from missing upstream context.
supporting_points: Leadership and portfolio artifacts already exist.
The current pipeline discards speaker notes and traceable evidence.
slides.speaker_notes: Make clear that the current presentation upgrade is an orchestration and rendering upgrade on top of existing artifact quality.
slides.audience_question_answered: Do we already have enough product context and source material to generate stronger decks?
slides.claim: ProductOS already has enough structured operating material; the quality loss happens in evidence packaging and story planning.
slides.why_now: Leadership should approve the current presentation-system investment and understand why this is a trust-and-execution upgrade rather than cosmetic work.
slides.proof_strategy: Show that upstream source artifacts already exist; Show where the current presentation pipeline compresses or drops useful structure
slides.transition_from_previous: Build from PM Operating System Update into Where We Are without breaking the recommendation thread.
slides.cta: Align on the architectural diagnosis before debating styling.

slides.title: What Still Needs Work
slides.headline: Without publish controls, better rhetoric could weaken trust.
slides.core_message: The system needs bounded roles and publish checks before it adds stronger narrative or design agents.
supporting_points: PPT export may require simpler layouts.
Risk and uncertainty must stay explicit in executive summaries.
slides.speaker_notes: Close the problem statement by connecting narrative improvement to governance and trust.
slides.audience_question_answered: What would make a stronger presentation engine trustworthy rather than dangerously persuasive?
slides.claim: Narrative upgrades are only acceptable if publish controls keep evidence, uncertainty, and export tradeoffs visible.
slides.why_now: Leadership should approve the current presentation-system investment and understand why this is a trust-and-execution upgrade rather than cosmetic work.
slides.proof_strategy: Show where publish controls are still thin; Show why confidence and fidelity treatment must stay explicit
slides.transition_from_previous: Build from Where We Are into What Still Needs Work without breaking the recommendation thread.
slides.cta: Treat publish checks and fidelity controls as part of the current presentation-system scope.
